subject: Cloud Computing
title: Time-Sharing: Computing by the Hour
description: A quick, practical guide to Time-Sharing: Computing by the Hour.

hook.text: Computers in the 1960s cost millions. An MIT professor had a radical idea: share the cost.

buildup.text: John McCarthy proposed time-sharing — one computer slicing its time among many users.

discovery.text: Each user got a tiny slice of processing time, but it felt like they had the whole machine.

twist.text: Time-sharing companies sold computing by the hour. They were the first cloud providers.

climax.text: The industry crashed in the 1980s when personal computers made shared access seem pointless.

punchline.text: Cheap PCs killed time-sharing. Then the internet revived it.

quiz.question: Why did the original time-sharing industry decline in the 1980s?
options: Personal computers became affordable, reducing need for shared access
Time-sharing was made illegal by the government
The internet replaced all time-sharing companies directly
